content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Seed defects in scope, robots, byte limits, selector cardinality, visited timing, abort, report escaping, and recovery and require tests to fail.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Mutation Detection model · Theory Mutation Detec…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Mutation Detection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Mutation Detection model · Theory Mutation Detec…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 4680 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove original fixtures, pure models, compile-negative tests, loopback faults, and mutation detection; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Seed defects in scope, robots, byte limits, selector cardinality, visited timing, abort, report escaping, and recovery and require tests to fail.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Mutation Detection model · Theory Mutation Detec…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: High statement coverage proves tests detect harmful changes.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-mutation-detection-1h4jjz.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-mutation-detection-1h4jjz(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_mutation_detection_1h4jjz\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?testCases < 18)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?detectedCases < 18 \|\| detectedCases > testCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?seededMutations < 8)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?survivingMutations > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
